Page.Width: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualWidth, FallbackValue=700}
Page.Height: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualHeight, FallbackValue=700}

Run.Text: This is an example of a flash precedence message sent to your task group from high command. NATO messages will generally follow the same formatting. All times given in these messages will be in Universal Coordinated Time (UTC), shown as 'Zulu time' or 'Z' in message date time blocks.

As you can see, this message indicates that one of our radar satellites passed over the North Pacific about an hour ago and detected the American convoy. You can now see that our search area marker has been greatly reduced in size. Additionally, the message informs us that our cruise missile submarine K-175 will be placed under our tactical command and will be available for the strike.
Let's now use our air assets to get a firm target location fix, and launch an attack using the long-range missiles aboard Varyag.
